name: per3-reader
description: Read per3 / DSI persistence values from shell scripts. Ships an OSGi bundle that installs into the DSITracer.jar slot on /mnt/efs-system/lsd/ — on MMI boot, the bundle registers as a DSIPersistenceListener and serves synchronous reads through a file-based trigger mechanism. Answers the long-standing 'how do I read per3 addresses without writing a Java GEM screen' question.
author: dspl1236
scripts: per3_install.sh - Install per3-reader.jar into /mnt/efs-system/lsd/ (requires MMI reboot)
per3_restore.sh - Remove the bundle, restore original DSITracer.jar if one was backed up
per3_read.sh - Shell client that issues reads through the bundle's file-trigger interface
usage: ksh ./scripts/per3_read.sh int 0x00000023     # battery voltage x 100 mV
how_it_works: 2. On next MMI boot, lsd.sh detects it and prepends to -Xbootclasspath, MODULAR=yes
3. The OSGi framework finds Bundle-Activator: de.dspl.per3reader.Activator
4. Activator opens a ServiceTracker on org.dsi.ifc.persistence.DSIPersistence
5. When DSIPersistence appears (DEVICE_NAME != *[FakePer]*), we wrap it in Per3Reader
6. Per3Reader is registered as an OSGi DSIPersistenceListener service so the DSI implementation routes callbacks to us
7. A TriggerLoop thread watches /fs/sda1/per3req/ for {ns}.{addr}.{type}.req files
8. Each request triggers an async DSI read, the callback wakes a per-address monitor, the result is written atomically to {ns}.{addr}.{type}.resp
risks: ALPHA: not yet verified on real hardware. Offline tests (9/9 passing) exercise the logic against an in-memory DSIPersistence mock, but the real dsi.jar interface may have additional methods we didn't stub, which would cause AbstractMethodError at class-load time. Run tools/extract_jars_from_efs.py against the firmware to cross-check before deploying.
Takes the DSITracer.jar slot — if you're actively debugging with DSITracer installed, this will replace it. per3_install.sh preserves the original as .bak so restoring is straightforward.
Requires MMI reboot to take effect (drops onto the bootclasspath at JVM start).
credits: DSIPersistence interface inferred from decompiled FakePersistence and PersistenceAccessor under research/decompiled/. Extension-point pattern (DSITracer.jar slot) documented by Harman-Becker in /mnt/efs-system/lsd/lsd.sh. Motivation and validation: DrGER2's 2026-04 review of this toolkit.
